children.title: We Love, We Remember, We Move On...
Basic Rules
text: IN SHORT
- Don't break shit.
- Don't steal shit.
- Don't kill without consent.
- Don't lag the server.
- Don't cheat without approval.
- Don't be an asshole.
BASIC RULES
1. Don't touch what isn't yours
Do not break, change, move, or rebuild other players' builds, farms, machines, roads, or decorations without the owner's permission.
2. Don't steal
Chests, barrels, shulker boxes, item frames, armor stands, displays, vending machines, and any other player-owned stuff are off-limits unless you have explicit permission from the owner.
3. PvP requires consent
Killing, traps, lava, fire, explosions, or any other way of harming a player are forbidden without that player's consent.
4. Don't ruin the game for others
Spam, toxicity, harassment, stalking, provocation, and deliberate annoyance are forbidden.
5. Don't trash the world
Do not leave dirt towers, random holes, half-cut trees, abandoned ruins, or other visual garbage near shared areas.
6. Don't lag the server
Lag machines, uncontrolled farms, mob piles, and mechanisms that noticeably tank TPS are forbidden.
7. Don't cheat
Cheats, x-ray, dupes, exploits, rule bypasses, and any unfair advantages are forbidden without prior approval.
8. Public means public
Spawn, hubs, roads, portals, quest areas, and public builds may not be claimed, broken, or modified without approval.
9. Don't exploit bugs
Found a bug, protection hole, or weird mod behavior? Report it to an admin. Using it for your own gain is forbidden.
10. Admins settle disputes
If the rules don't cover a situation, an admin makes the call. Discussion is fine. Drama is not.

children.title: Player Bill of Rights
text: IN SHORT
- Right to a peaceful, cozy game.
- Right to personal property.
- Right to a home and home defense.
- Right to bear arms.
- Right to assemble.
- Right to fair trade.
- Right to refuse.
- Right to admin help.
- Right to be heard.
- There is no right to elect the admin.
PLAYER BILL OF RIGHTS
I. Right to a Peaceful Game
Players have the right to play without griefing, theft, harassment, stalking, toxicity, or non-consensual killing.
II. Right to Property
A player's builds, farms, machines, items, animals, shops, and reasonably claimed land belong to that player.
III. Right to a Home
Players have the right to build a base and call it home, as long as it does not claim public areas or interfere with other projects.
IV. Right to Defend the Home
On their own land, players may defend themselves, their property, and their home. The server recognizes a Texas-flavored Castle Doctrine, no bullshit bureaucracy attached.
V. Right to Bear Arms
Players have the right to own weapons, armor, traps, and defensive mechanisms, as long as they are not used for griefing, terror, or non-consensual killing.
VI. Right to Assemble
Players may gather, team up, form towns, markets, clubs, cults, and other suspicious organizations, as long as they do not break server rules.
VII. Right to Trade
Players may sell, buy, barter, and set their own prices, as long as every deal is honest and voluntary.
VIII. Right to Refuse
Players are not required to join PvP, quests, trades, builds, events, or anyone else's plans against their will.
IX. Right to Admin Help
Players may ask an admin for help with disputes, bugs, theft, griefing, conflicts, or technical problems.
X. Right to Be Heard
Before punishment, a player has the right to explain their side, unless the situation is obvious.
XI. Right to Damage Recovery
If a player suffers loss from a bug, griefing, or admin error, they may request reasonable compensation.
XII. Right to Create
Players may build, decorate, and develop their projects in their own style, as long as it does not ruin the game for others.
XIII. Right to Equal Rules
The rules apply equally to all players, except when Great Pazuzu has already made the call.
XIV. Right Not to Elect the Admin
Players have no right to elect, re-elect, remove, impeach, audit, or question the legitimacy of the server admin.

children.title: Duties of Players
text: IN SHORT
- Players must respect Domestia, its flag, symbols, and official documents.
- Players must keep public places clean and usable.
- Players must clean up their own mess.
- Players must report bugs, dupes, and protection holes.
- Players must trade honestly.
- Players must fix damage they caused.
- Players must not drag others into unwanted bullshit.
- Players must keep their builds server-friendly.
- Players must accept admin decisions.
DUTIES OF PLAYERS
I. Duty to Respect Domestia
Players must respect Domestia, its name, flag, symbols, official documents, and public institutions. Mockery is allowed. Desecration is not.
II. Duty to Protect Public Places
Spawn, roads, portals, hubs, quest areas, markets, and public builds must remain clean, accessible, and functional.
III. Duty to Clean Up
Players must remove temporary blocks, dirt towers, random holes, half-cut trees, abandoned junk, and other crap they leave behind.
IV. Duty to Mark Danger
Players must mark or secure dangerous holes, lava, traps, portals, farms, and machines that may accidentally kill people or wreck someone's day.
V. Duty to Report Problems
Players must report bugs, dupes, protection holes, lag sources, and weird mod behavior to an admin instead of exploiting that shit.
VI. Duty of Honest Trade
Players must make goods, prices, and deal terms clear. Scams, bug-trading, and shady bullshit are not service to Domestia.
VII. Duty to Repair Damage
If a player breaks, floods, burns, blows up, steals, or otherwise fucks something up by accident, they must own it and help repair the damage.
VIII. Duty to Respect Other Players' Peace
Players must not force PvP, quests, trades, builds, events, religions, cults, or other nonsense onto people who do not want in.
IX. Duty to Protect Server Performance
Players must keep farms, mobs, machines, storage systems, and redstone from turning Domestia into a slideshow.
X. Duty to Maintain What You Use
If players use a public road, portal, farm, market, or hub, they must not make it worse and should fix obvious small problems when they can.
XI. Duty to Speak Facts
In disputes, players must explain what happened instead of starting drama, tantrums, or courtroom cosplay made of mud and sticks.
XII. Duty to Accept Admin Judgment
After review, players must accept the admin's decision, even if their inner goblin strongly disagrees.
LIMIT OF DUTIES
No duty cancels player rights, forces anyone to give up personal property, requires unwanted participation, or permits violation of the Basic Rules.
